name: Protein Ice Cream Sandwich
description: Healthy protein ice cream sandwiches in the style of Fat Boys: a blended vanilla protein 'ice cream' between chocolate graham crackers. Source: healthbeet.org. Decent as-is; the filling really wants a Ninja Creami churn for texture. Stated macros ~135 kcal / 16C / 13-14P / 3F per sandwich.

steps.instructionText: Blend the protein powder, almond milk, pudding mix, Greek yogurt, vanilla, and salt smooth in a blender.
Freeze ~6 hours, until mostly frozen. Re-churn in a Ninja Creami (best) or re-blend in a food processor.
Line a cake pan with parchment and arrange half-sheets of graham cracker, 6 across the bottom.
Spread the ice cream over the crackers until all are covered.
Top with a second graham cracker and return to the freezer until frozen. Slice into 6 sandwiches.

notes.text: If you have a Ninja Creami: freeze 24 hr (not 6) for the best churn — the full freeze gives a denser base that re-processes into a creamier texture; the 6-hr option works but comes out icier.

Don't skip the re-churn step. Straight from the first freeze the mix is an icy slab, not spreadable — it needs the Creami or a food-processor pulse to become soft enough to spread over the crackers without cracking them.
Work fast spreading the ice cream over the 6 graham halves — it melts quickly at room temp and a thin spread = structurally weak sandwiches. Pre-chill the pan and crackers 10 min before assembly.

Return to freezer immediately after topping with the second crackers and freeze until FULLY set (2+ hr) before slicing — cutting warm gives you compressed, stuck-together sandwiches. Use a hot dry knife to slice cleanly.
Pull sandwiches 3-5 min before eating; frozen solid they are hard enough to crack the graham cracker on first bite. A short temper softens just the filling while keeping the cracker snap.
